entry.title: BIP 119 CTV activation client
entry.summary:
Following the unanimous agreement on the activation parameters previously discussed, significant updates have been made to enhance accessibility and understanding regarding the deployment of CTV (BIP-119). The website now features a direct link to the activation client's repository, ensuring that interested parties can easily access the necessary tools and information for engagement. This addition is aimed at facilitating smoother transitions and implementations for users and developers alike. The repository can be found at [https://github.com/ctv-activation/activation-client](https://github.com/ctv-activation/activation-client).
To further support this initiative, a series of Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) has been compiled and published on the website. These FAQs aim to address common inquiries and concerns, providing clear and concise answers to aid understanding of the activation process and its implications. This effort underscores the commitment to transparency and effective communication throughout this update's rollout.
The deployment details for CTV (BIP-119) were explicitly outlined, marking important milestones and technical specifications crucial for the implementation phase. The deployment is scheduled to start on 30 March 2026, with an end date set for 30 March 2027. A minimum activation height of 1001952 is expected by May 2027, indicating the depth of preparation and planning that has gone into ensuring a seamless transition. The consensus mechanism requires a 90% threshold over a period of 2016, establishing a robust framework for achieving widespread agreement and support within the community.
Additionally, an archive link has been provided ([archive.is/o6QgT](https://archive.is/o6QgT)) for those interested in accessing a snapshot of the project's progress and documentation. This resource serves as a valuable point of reference for historical data and decision-making processes related to the CTV deployment.
In summary, these updates and resources mark significant progress in the CTV (BIP-119) deployment, offering comprehensive guidance and information to all stakeholders involved. Through meticulous planning, transparent communication, and strategic deployment schedules, the project aims to achieve successful implementation and widespread adoption within the specified timeframe.
